name: ODE-Based Dynamic Attribute Valuation with Cognitive Control
description: Continuous-time ODE system (Euler-discretized) where attribute weights, hunger state, and a cognitive control (self-regulation) variable co-evolve. The cognitive control variable K represents dlPFC engagement that dynamically up-weights abstract/quality attributes, creating a self-regulation mechanism. When K is high, the agent favors high-palatability food (long-term quality); when K depletes, behavior shifts toward proximity-seeking (immediate reward). Attribute weights track equilibrium targets via exponential smoothing, introducing inertia — the agent cannot instantly switch behavioral strategies. A Q-learning component captures environmental structure and is blended with attribute-based values for action selection. Grounded in Rangel (2013) on dlPFC top-down modulation (P3, P5) and Rangel, Camerer & Montague (2008) on dual goal-directed/habitual systems.

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Internal physiological hunger drive. Rises slowly each step (metabolic cost), drops by R_food when food is consumed. Drives up the equilibrium target for w_imm.

variables.name: cognitive_control
variables.description: Self-regulation / dlPFC engagement level. Passively recovers toward resting level K_0, depleted by c_K each decision step. Higher K boosts the abstract attribute weight.

variables.name: immediate_weight
variables.description: Dynamic weight for the immediate (proximity/hedonic) attribute. Tracks equilibrium target w_imm_star via exponential smoothing with time constant tau_w.

variables.name: abstract_weight
variables.description: Dynamic weight for the abstract (palatability/quality) attribute. Tracks equilibrium target w_abs_star. Always sums with w_imm to approximately 1.

variables.name: immediate_weight_target
variables.description: Equilibrium target for w_imm, computed from competition between hunger (favoring immediate) and cognitive control (favoring abstract).

variables.name: abstract_weight_target
variables.description: Equilibrium target for w_abs, complement of w_imm_star.

variables.name: option_value
variables.description: Attribute-based value of an option (resource), computed as weighted sum of immediate and abstract attribute values.

variables.name: q_value
variables.description: Learned action-value from TD(0) Q-learning. Captures environmental structure (e.g., which directions tend to lead to rewards). Indexed by (state_bin, action).

variables.name: blended_action_value
variables.description: Final action value blending attribute-based V(o_a) with learned Q(a). Equal weighting: U(a) = 0.5 * V(o_a) + 0.5 * Q(a).

variables.name: immediate_attribute_value
variables.description: Proximity-based desirability of the resource targeted by an action. Same mapping as other formulations.

variables.name: abstract_attribute_value
variables.description: Quality/palatability attribute of the targeted resource. Derived from food palatability property.

parameters.name: cognitive_control_recovery_time_constant
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): dlPFC engagement is effortful and recovers slowly

parameters.name: control_depletion_per_step
parameters.source: Models ego-depletion / cognitive cost of abstract attribute processing (Rangel 2013)

parameters.name: control_boost_to_abstract_weight
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): dlPFC amplifies health/abstract attribute weighting

parameters.name: weight_dynamics_time_constant
parameters.source: Determines how quickly weights track state changes; moderate inertia

rules.description: Hunger dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE). Hunger rises by eta_H each step and drops by R_food when food is consumed. Clipped to [0, 1].
rules.pseudocode:
# dH/dt = eta_H - R_food * ate
ate = 1 if (action == 'eat' and consumed) else 0
H_t = clip(H_t + eta_H - R_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)

rules.description: Cognitive control dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE). K passively recovers toward resting level K_0 with time constant tau_K, and is depleted by c_K every step (representing the ongoing cost of deliberation).
rules.pseudocode:
# dK/dt = (K_0 - K) / tau_K - c_K
K_t = clip(K_t + (K_0 - K_t) / tau_K - c_K, 0.0, 1.0)

rules.description: Compute equilibrium target weights from hunger-control competition. Hunger pushes w_imm_star up; cognitive control pushes w_abs_star up. Targets always sum to 1.
rules.pseudocode:
denom = 2.0 + H_t + phi * K_t
w_imm_star = (1.0 + H_t) / denom
w_abs_star = (1.0 + phi * K_t) / denom

rules.description: Weight dynamics (Euler-discretized exponential tracking ODE). Weights smoothly track their targets with time constant tau_w, introducing inertia.
rules.pseudocode:
# dw_i/dt = (w_i_star - w_i) / tau_w
w_imm = w_imm + (w_imm_star - w_imm) / tau_w
w_abs = w_abs + (w_abs_star - w_abs) / tau_w

rules.description: Compute attribute values for each candidate action. Immediate attribute = proximity to nearest food. Abstract attribute = palatability-derived quality.
Option value as weighted linear sum of attribute values using current (inertia-smoothed) weights.
Blended action value combining attribute-based value (goal-directed) with learned Q-value (experiential). Equal weighting reflects dual-system arbitration from Rangel, Camerer & Montague (2008).
Softmax action selection over blended action values.
rules.pseudocode:
for each action a:
  logit_a = beta * U_a
max_logit = max(logits)
for each action a:
  prob_a = exp(logit_a - max_logit)
prob = prob / sum(prob)
selected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=prob)

rules.description: Q-value update via TD(0) Q-learning after action execution and observation of reward and new state.
rules.pseudocode:
# State discretization for Q-table: bin position into (x, y) cells
state = (perception['x'], perception['y'])
new_state = (new_perception['x'], new_perception['y'])
max_Q_next = max(Q[(new_state, a_prime)] for a_prime in all_actions)
Q[(state, selected_action)] += alpha_Q * (reward + gamma * max_Q_next - Q[(state, selected_action)])

decision_logic.description: Each simulation step: (1) Read perception for position, food, and derive internal state. (2) Update ODEs: hunger (R1), cognitive control (R2), weight targets (R3), weights (R4) — this is the Euler integration step. (3) For each action, compute attribute values (R5), option values (R6). (4) Retrieve Q-values and compute blended values (R7). (5) Select action via softmax (R8). After execution: (6) Update Q-values via TD learning (R9). Key differentiator: the ODE dynamics create temporal inertia in weights, and cognitive control depletion causes gradual drift from quality-seeking to proximity-seeking behavior over extended decision episodes.
pseudocode: # R3: Compute target weights from hunger-control competition
# R5: Compute attribute values for each action
food_at_pos = [f for f in food_list if f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1]]
# --- UPDATE PHASE (called after action execution with reward, new_perception) ---
max_Q_next = max(Q_table.get((new_state_key, a), 0.0) for a in actions)
Q_table[(state_key, selected_action)] = current_Q + alpha_Q * (reward + gamma * max_Q_next - current_Q)

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {'x': int, 'y': int, 'type': 'food', 'palatability': float}
perception_to_variables.food_palatability: resource['palatability'] for each resource in food_sources
perception_to_variables.step: perception['step']  # used for tracking Q-table state

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action -> ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 on successful consumption)

expected_behaviors.description: Cognitive control depletes over sustained decision-making, causing gradual shift from quality-seeking to proximity-seeking behavior.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 200 steps without eating -> assert K_t < K_0; assert w_imm > w_abs; assert w_imm increased from initial value

expected_behaviors.description: After a period of rest (not depleting K much), cognitive control recovers and the agent again prefers high-palatability food even if further away.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: deplete K to 0.1 then run 100 steps with easy food -> assert K_t recovers toward K_0; assert w_abs increases

expected_behaviors.description: Weight inertia (tau_w) means sudden changes in hunger or K do not instantly reconfigure behavior — there is a smooth transition period.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set H_t = 0.9 suddenly (was 0.2) -> assert w_imm does not jump to new equilibrium immediately; assert |w_imm(t+1) - w_imm(t)| < |w_imm_star - w_imm(t)|

expected_behaviors.description: Q-learning component allows the agent to learn spatial structure (e.g., food tends to appear in certain areas), complementing the attribute-based computation.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 500 steps in stable environment -> assert Q-values near food locations are higher; assert agent navigates toward food more efficiently over time

expected_behaviors.description: Hungry agent (high H_t) with depleted cognitive control (low K_t) strongly prefers the nearest food regardless of palatability.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set H_t=0.95, K_t=0.05, two foods: near(low palatability) and far(high palatability) -> assert agent moves toward near food > 70% of trials

expected_behaviors.description: Agent eats food when on the same cell, as the blended value of eat is high (V_o has a_imm=1.0 and Q learns positive value for eating).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place agent on food, H_t=0.5, K_t=0.5 -> assert eat action selected with high probability

references: Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717-1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561
Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9(7), 545-556. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357
